request.question: In between The Postal Service and Chagall Guevara which one was formed in 1989?

title: Afghan Post
Postage stamps and postal history of Poland
Chagall Guevara (album)
United States Postal Inspection Service
Army Postal Service (India)
Chagall Guevara
SpeedPost
Canada Post
Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil
The Postal Service
sentences: Afghan Post is the national post office of Afghanistan.
It is responsible for providing postal service in Afghanistan.
Afghanistan first established a postal service in 1870, where it received international recognition.
On the eve of the Soviet invasion it had grown into one of the stronger regional postal services, able to send and receive letters from anywhere in the world in a timely manner.
During the 1990s the Afghan postal service lay in near total ruin, undone by the nation's civil war.
Sending a letter usually meant having to find someone traveling in the direction of the recipient willing to carry a note and hoping for the best.
Since the civil war, the postal service has reinvented itself with offices in all 34 provinces, and, it is close to having offices in all 364 districts.

As the government struggles to develop despite an excess of foreign aid, the post office has quietly managed to become one of the most efficient national institutions – and with extremely limited international assistance.
Though street addresses are a foreign concept in Afghanistan, the postal service manages to do house deliveries.
The Afghan postal service has turned into a promising administration.
Poczta Polska, the Polish postal service, was founded in 1558 and postal markings were first introduced in 1764.
The three partitions of Poland in 1772, 1793 and 1795 saw the independent nation of Poland disappear.
The postal services in the areas occupied by Germany and Austria were absorbed into those countries' postal services.
In 1772 the area occupied by Austria was created into the Kingdom of Galicia, a part of the Austrian Empire.
This lasted till 1918.
The Duchy of Warsaw was created briefly, between 1807 and 1813, by Napoleon I of France, from Polish lands ceded by the Kingdom of Prussia under the terms of the Treaties of Tilsit.
In 1815, following Napoleons’ defeat in 1813, the Congress of Vienna, created Congress Poland out of the Duchy of Warsaw and also established the Free City of Kraków.
Congress Poland was placed under the control of Russia and the postal service was given autonomy in 1815.
In 1851 the postal service was put under the control of the Russian post office department regional office in St Petersburg.
In 1855 control was restored for a while to the Congress Kingdom but following the uprising in 1863 again came under Russian control from 1866 and continued until World War I.
In November 1918 the Second Polish Republic was created.
Chagall Guevara is the title of the only full-length album by the band Chagall Guevara, released in 1991, on MCA Records.
The United States Postal Inspection Service (USPIS) is the law enforcement arm of the United States Postal Service.
Its jurisdiction is defined as "crimes that may adversely affect or fraudulently use the U.S. Mail, the postal system or postal employees."

The mission of the U.S. Postal Inspection Service is to support and protect the U.S. Postal Service, its employees, infrastructure, and customers by enforcing the laws that defend the nation’s mail system from illegal or dangerous use.
The Army Postal Service (APS), functions as a government-operated military mail system in India.

A primary feature of Army Postal Service systems is that normally they are subsidized to ensure that military mail posted between duty stations abroad and the home country (or vice versa) does not cost the sender any more than normal domestic mail traffic.

In some cases, Indian military personnel in a combat zone may post letters and/or packages to the home country for free, while in others, senders located in a specific overseas area may send military mail to another military recipient, also located in the same overseas area, without charge.
Officers are deputed primarily from the Indian Postal Service and from the Army Corps.
Army Postal Service is the only service that enables civil service officers to work in the armed forces.

Chagall Guevara was an American rock band formed in 1989 by solo artist Steve Taylor, guitarists Dave Perkins and Lynn Nichols (from the 1970s Phil Keaggy band), bassist Wade Jaynes, and drummer Mike Mead.
SpeedPost or Speed Post is the trade name or brand name for a high-speed postal service provided by several postal companies around the world.

It is Taiwan Post's high-speed domestic postal service in Taiwan, Hongkong Post's international courier post service (with the generic name "EMS"), India Post's local and international postal as well as money transfer service, as well as SingPost's local and international express and trackable postal and courier service

Canada Post Corporation, known more simply as Canada Post (French: "Société Canadienne des Postes" , or simply "Postes Canada"), is a Crown corporation which functions as the country's primary postal operator.

Originally known as Royal Mail Canada (the operating name of the Post Office Department of the Canadian government founded in 1867), rebranding was done to the “Canada Post” name in the late 1960s, even though it had not yet been separated from the government.
On October 16, 1981, the Canada Post Corporation Act came into effect.
This abolished the Post Office Department and created the present day Crown corporation which provides postal service.
The act aimed to set a new direction for the postal service by ensuring the postal service's financial security and independence.

Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil is an American alternative rock supergroup formed in 2010 by singer Steve Taylor with drummer Peter Furler (Newsboys, Peter Furler Band), guitarist Jimmy Abegg (Vector, A Ragamuffin Band), and bassist John Mark Painter (Fleming and John).
Taylor formerly saw success as a new wave singer during the 1980s and early 1990s, and also fronted the short-lived band Chagall Guevara.
However, by the mid-1990s he abandoned performing music and transitioned into work as a record producer, songwriter, and film-maker.

In 2010 Taylor, who was frustrated at the slow pace of his fundraiser for his feature film "Blue Like Jazz", collaborated with Furler, Abegg, and Painter to record the song "A Life Preserved" for the film's soundtrack, and began recording other material.
Taylor, Abegg, and Painter then featured on the Peter Furler song "Closer", under the name "Steve Taylor & Some Other Band", on Furler's album "On Fire".
With those collaborations as a starting point, Taylor relaunched his career as a performing musician, and the lineup was branded as Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil.
The group released a studio album entitled "Goliath" on November 18, 2014 to critical acclaim and modest commercial success.
In 2015, Daniel Smith of Danielson, with whom the band had been touring, temporarily joined on in a collaboration touted as Steve Taylor & The Danielson Foil.
Under this incarnation, the group released the EP "Wow to the Deadness" on February 5, 2016, and toured until February 12.

The Postal Service were an American indie rock band from Seattle, Washington, consisted of vocalist Ben Gibbard (of Death Cab for Cutie), producer Jimmy Tamborello (of Dntel and Headset), with Jenny Lewis (of Rilo Kiley) on background vocals.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: In between The Postal Service and Chagall Guevara which one was formed in 1989?
hop1_docs: Chagall Guevara | Chagall Guevara was an American rock band formed in 1989 by solo artist Steve Taylor, guitarists Dave Perkins and Lynn Nichols (from the 1970s Phil Keaggy band), bassist Wade Jaynes, and drummer Mike Mead.
Chagall Guevara (album) | Chagall Guevara is the title of the only full-length album by the band Chagall Guevara, released in 1991, on MCA Records.

List of postal codes in Brazil | This list of postal codes in Brazil covers Brazil's Código de Endereçamento Postal (CEP), which is the Brazilian postal code.  In 1993, the eight-digit form was introduced; prior to 1993, the Brazilian postal codes used the five-digit form.  Correios is the state-owned company which provides postal services in Brazil.
Liver (album) | Liver is a live album by Steve Taylor, released in 1995.  Its contents cover all of Taylor's career, including his time with Chagall Guevara.

Hrvatska pošta | Croatian Post Inc. (HP-Hrvatska pošta d.d.), founded in 1999, is a business corporation in ownership of the Republic of Croatia.  Croatian Post is a national postal operator of the Republic of Croatia.  Post offices of the Croatian Post (total number: 1016) form one of the widest services and retail networks in the country.  Apart from postal services Croatian Post offers also payment services, retail services and digital TV service in the whole territory of Croatia.  Croatian Post is also one of the founders and a full member of the Association of European Public Postal Operators (PostEurop), while Croatia is a member of the Universal Postal Union (UPU).  Croatian Post also issues postage stamps of Croatia.

Sectional center facility | A destination Sectional Center Facility (SCF) is a Processing and Distribution Center (P&DC) of the United States Postal Service (USPS) that serves a designated geographical area defined by one or more three-digit ZIP Code prefixes.  A Sectional Center Facility routes mail between local post offices and to and from Network Distribution Centers (NDCs), which form the backbone of the network.

Vectis postal service | The Vectis postal service was a postal service set up during the national postal strike of the United Kingdom in 1971.  This was amongst a number of local delivery services which flourished during the strike.
hop1_titles: List of postal codes in Brazil
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found:
  - Chagall Guevara: passage [1] ("Chagall Guevara | ...") contains the phrase "was an American rock band formed in 1989" (quote: "formed in 1989") and lists band members (e.g., "Steve Taylor") — see passage [1].
  - The Postal Service: no passage in the provided set mentions "The Postal Service" (the band). Several postal-related pages appear (passages [3], [5], [6], [7]) but they are institutional/postal-service topics, not the band "The Postal Service".
- Ambiguities:
  - "The Postal Service" could refer to the band or to generic/official postal organizations. The specific band page/title "The Postal Service (band)" is not present among the provided passages. Candidate non-band pages present: "List of postal codes in Brazil" [3], "Hrvatska pošta" [5], "Sectional center facility" [6], "Vectis postal service" [7].
  - No multiple candidate band pages for "The Postal Service" appear in these passages; the expected band page is missing.
- Bridge entity / Missing fact:
  - Bridge entity (as given in passages): "Chagall Guevara" (passage [1]).
  - Missing fact needed to answer the question: the formation year (or "formed" / "years active") for "The Postal Service" (the band).
- Retrieval direction (prioritized next hops):
  1) Open "The Postal Service (band)" page — check the lede/infobox for "formed" or "years active" and the "History" / "Background" section for formation year. (Search hint: "The Postal Service band formed" or "The Postal Service years active".)
  2) If the band page is ambiguous or missing, open "The Postal Service (disambiguation)" to locate the band article or variant titles, then check that article's lede/infobox.
  3) Re-open "Chagall Guevara" (passage [1]) page to confirm the quoted "formed in 1989" in the lede/infobox if needed.
  4) If necessary, check band discography or biography sections for explicit "formed in <year>" phrasing.
Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved.
